local.summary: One private user, Apple Silicon, offline preferred, 32K context, low operational overhead.

production.summary: One hundred concurrent users, NVIDIA GPU service, strict p95 latency, shared model endpoint.

fit.local: Apple-native execution and unified-memory fit make it the first candidate for this local workload.
fit.production: It does not match the NVIDIA multi-user production target or its request-scheduling needs.

fit.local: Portable GGUF execution, privacy, and CPU/Metal flexibility fit one offline user.
fit.production: It can serve concurrent traffic, but this lesson needs the production scheduling and KV observability of vLLM.

fit.local: The easiest local product surface, but it hides some low-level controls needed for this lesson.
fit.production: Model management alone does not satisfy the shared-service scheduling and evidence requirements.

fit.local: Production scheduling complexity adds little value for one private laptop user.
fit.production: Continuous batching, paged KV, prefix reuse, metrics, and multi-GPU paths fit the concurrent service.

fit.local: Its production runtime controls are excessive for this single-user local path.
fit.production: A strong candidate for prefix-heavy agent workloads, but the Nebius live path is not yet validated in this build.
